overview: Add a repository-native XML retrieval mode to `c8volt get process-definition` by reusing the existing versioned processdefinition XML service capability, keeping stdout safe for shell redirection, preserving current non-XML behavior, and finishing with focused tests, generated docs, and repository validation.

userStories.title: Expose process definition XML through the public facade
userStories.description: Extend the existing `c8volt/process` API and client wiring so the CLI can request process definition XML through the same repository-native facade used for other process-definition operations.
acceptanceCriteria: The public process API in `c8volt/process/api.go` includes one XML retrieval method for a single process definition.
The process client in `c8volt/process/client.go` delegates XML retrieval to the existing versioned processdefinition service surface without introducing a new command-only path.
The resulting wiring remains compatible with the current branch `69-process-definition-xml` and existing repository structure.
userStories.notes: Keep this slice limited to shared wiring. Reuse the existing service patterns and avoid changing package layout or introducing a parallel abstraction.

userStories.title: Add the XML command path for a single process definition
userStories.description: Implement `c8volt get process-definition --key <key> --xml` so a successful call emits only the raw XML payload for one explicitly selected definition while leaving non-XML behavior unchanged.
acceptanceCriteria: The command handler in `cmd/get_processdefinition.go` supports XML retrieval only when a single process definition is explicitly selected with `--key`.
A successful XML request writes only the XML payload to stdout through the dedicated command path, without list summaries or JSON wrapping.
Existing non-XML `get process-definition` list and detail behavior still works when `--xml` is not used.
userStories.notes: The upstream issue mentions `--id`, but the current CLI surface uses `--key`; keep the implementation aligned with the existing command contract unless a separate rename is approved.

userStories.title: Make XML mode script-safe and fully verified
userStories.description: Tighten XML flag validation, preserve predictable failure semantics, and add focused regression coverage so redirection and error handling work cleanly in automation.
acceptanceCriteria: XML mode rejects incompatible filter or render combinations in `cmd/get_processdefinition.go` instead of silently choosing precedence.

Focused tests cover XML success, retrieval failure, redirect-safe stdout behavior, and any needed facade or versioned service regression cases in `cmd/get_test.go`, `internal/services/processdefinition/v87/service_test.go`, and `internal/services/processdefinition/v88/service_test.go`.
Validation includes targeted Go test runs for the affected command and processdefinition packages.
userStories.notes: Prefer tests that verify observable command behavior. Follow the repository conventions for temp config usage, explicit Camunda version selection in tests, and subprocess assertions when command failures exit via `os.Exit`.

userStories.title: Update help text, regenerate docs, and finish repository validation
userStories.description: Document the XML workflow through command help and generated CLI docs, adjust user-facing examples only where needed, and complete the final repository validation sequence.
acceptanceCriteria: Help text in `cmd/get_processdefinition.go` describes the XML option and its single-definition usage constraints.

Generated CLI reference output under `docs/cli/` is regenerated from command metadata and reflects the XML workflow, and any needed `README.md` or `docs/index.md` examples stay consistent with shipped behavior.
Final validation includes `make docs` and `make test`.
userStories.notes: Do not hand-edit generated CLI docs beyond the repository's normal generation flow. Keep documentation changes tightly scoped to the user-visible XML workflow.
